items.title: Cross-border child protection hub aims to keep kids connected to families

children.content: A new hub to support families caught between three child protection agencies has been established in Alice Springs in a 12-month trial.

image.altText: Three Indigenous women sit side by side on chairs in a room and lean into each other for a photo.

items.title: NT child protection bill opponents show 'moral bankruptcy', MLA says

children.content: Fierce debate has surrounded the Northern Territory government's child protection reforms — which will return to parliament for debate next week — with one MLA saying those opposing it have shown "a degree of moral bankruptcy".

image.altText: A man in a navy jacket and pink tie gestures with his hands as he talks

items.title: NT government slammed over no-show to federal racism inquiry

children.content: A national inquiry into racism against First Nations people, launched after an alleged terror attack in Perth, has heard allegations of systemic racism in the Northern Territory, as the territory government comes under fire for snubbing the probe.

items.title: Hundreds face homelessness as accommodation centre set to close

children.content: Hundreds of residents fear they could be left homeless after the Northern Territory government announced it will shut a Darwin short-stay accommodation centre in less than 90 days.

image.altText: A young father and his son look at the camera.

items.title: 'I want the money that is owed to me': Charity fails to pay $150k in super

children.content: Former staff of charity PAWS Darwin are speaking out about being short-changed on their superannuation to the tune of $150,000. Under-18 staff also say the super accounts PAWS promised to create for them never existed.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a white woman, with blonde hair tied back, black singlet top, sitting among greenery.

items.title: Tourists turn away from NT as Kakadu travellers greeted by closure signs

children.content: It is known as the jewel in the Northern Territory's tourism crown, but visitors to Kakadu National Park are being greeted by closure signs blocking roads to key waterfall sites.

image.altText: View of Twin Falls at Kakadu National Park.

items.title: Health department refuses to move staff out of mould-riddled building

children.content: A Darwin building with a community health clinic inside has had to close off areas due to a years-long mould infestation across two floors.

image.altText: A three storey white building with a sign saying 'Casuarina Plaza'

items.title: 'Haven't had a day off all year': PM defends broken festival promise

children.content: A year ago the Anthony Albanese vowed to attend the Garma Festival every year for as long as he remained prime minister. Now he says he needs a break.

gallery.altText: Anthony Albanese, wearing a hat and no tie, addresses a crowd.

items.title: Former diplomat complains of Triple Zero outages on Telstra's 'priority' service

children.content: Telstra promised Alastair Gaisford that his wife's healthcare was a priority. But he says 15 landline outages, including some that could have prevented the ability to call Triple Zero in an emergency, have convinced him otherwise.

articles.title: Telstra CEO to be grilled over nationwide outage and compensation

children.content: Telstra CEO Vicki Brady is set to be grilled over the nationwide outage, and potential compensation. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

children.content: Vaping and smoking rates are on the decline, but a shift to other nicotine products could represent an increase in average use.

items.title: 'They were not random attacks': The tankers targeted in the Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The commercial tankers struck as they transited the Strait of Hormuz in recent weeks were not random, but part of a "highly, highly targeted" campaign run by Tehran, according to marine traffic watchers.

image.altText: A black, red and white oil tanker on dark ocean water and under a blue sky.

items.title: Teleprompter operator suspended over bets on Trump's speeches

children.content: US President Donald Trump's longtime technician allegedly made over $142,000 by placing bets on what Donald Trump would say in speeches.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaking at a lectern with two teleprompters in front of him.

items.title: 'Best place to look for life': Atmosphere discovered on Earth-like planet

children.content: The search for life beyond Earth has marked a new discovery — evidence of an atmosphere on an Earth-like planet beyond our Solar System with modelling suggesting it may be a water world.

items.title: This discovery in a Tokyo bookshop made me run away to the mountains

items.title: How a compulsive list maker ended up making 'extraordinary' observations

children.content: Mike Connor visited the same park for 40 years and wrote down what he saw. He didn't realise he was incidentally chronicling a changing environment and contributing to science.

items.title: Owning a home in Sydney has become a pipe dream for many, how about other states?

items.title: 'Substantial clean-up effort' needed for shuttered manganese smelter

children.content: Australia's only manganese alloy smelter has closed, leaving the future of the site uncertain and the country more reliant on imported alloys for steel production.

items.title: Uber reverses 'disability tax' after outcry

children.content: Uber has reversed its decision to impose a $5 fee on passengers requiring extra assistance, after advocates and service providers labelled it discriminatory and a "disability tax".

image.altText: An uber sign on a car.

items.title: Revered surfboard designer and intersex advocate dies in house fire

items.title: Father 'disgusted' over inadequacy of charges after Laos methanol poisoning

items.title: One of leading causes of infant deaths sees 'fantastic' decline in NSW

items.title: AI needs more memory. South Korea is selling it at a price
